source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Consider a small air pistol with a cylinder volume of 1 cm3 at 250 kPa, 27\circ\text{C}. The bullet acts as a piston initially held by a trigger. The bullet is released, so the air expands in an adiabatic process. If the pressure should be 120 kPa as the bullet leaves the cylinder, find the work done by the air.

The bullet is released, so the air expands in an adiabatic process. If the pressure should be 120 kPa as the bullet leaves the cylinder, find the work done by the air.
